danger_signals.visual_form: darkened/abandoned buildings with visual decay treatment
danger_signals.meaning: neighborhood deterioration due to poor services, low land value, or economic collapse

danger_signals.visual_form: darkened power plant structures; query reveals low power consumption percentage or aging warning
danger_signals.meaning: critical infrastructure reaching end-of-life; blackout risk imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: red-highlighted budget interface; negative treasury indicators
danger_signals.meaning: unsustainable spending leading to game-over condition if uncorrected

danger_signals.visual_form: environmental damage visible on map; affected zones show destruction
danger_signals.meaning: natural or catastrophic event (fire, flood, etc.) affecting city stability

danger_signals.visual_form: scaffolding and partial building graphics in residential/commercial/industrial zones
danger_signals.meaning: infrastructure development in progress; temporary economic drag or opportunity

safety_signals.visual_form: fully rendered buildings, power plants, hospitals, schools, libraries with standard color/design
safety_signals.meaning: essential services operational and accessible to population

safety_signals.visual_form: balanced distribution of residential, commercial, and industrial zones with visual variety
safety_signals.meaning: economic health; diversified tax base and employment opportunities

safety_signals.visual_form: query tool reveals elevated land value; well-maintained buildings; good connectivity visible
safety_signals.meaning: desirable neighborhood; population satisfaction and stability

safety_signals.visual_form: visible road networks, highways, railways, subway tunnels, bus depots, seaports, airports
safety_signals.meaning: functional mobility and trade enabling economic growth
financial stability allowing expansion and emergency response

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: darkened/abandoned residential structures indicate decay or depopulation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: residential zone areas; population density reflected in building density and variation

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: empty commercial lots; derelict commercial buildings indicating business failure
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: commercial zones near transportation hubs; visual clustering indicates economic activity

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: inactive or polluting industrial buildings; visual degradation of surrounding environment
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: industrial zones typically isolated; proximity to power plants and rail; environmental impact visible

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: institutional buildings (hospitals, schools, libraries, museums, prisons, fire/police stations)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: understaffed or deteriorating service buildings; query reveals reduced coverage
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: strategic placement for service radius coverage; density indicates city development stage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: sparse building placement; extensive empty terrain; visible construction scaffolding; basic road networks
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: slow population growth; limited tax revenue; budget constraints; high individual decision impact

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: increasing building density; mix of completed and under-construction structures; expanding infrastructure networks; improved road connectivity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: rising population; increasing tax revenue; new service demands; infrastructure competition for resources

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: high building density; complete infrastructure; diverse facility types; visible neighborhoods with distinct character; established power distribution and water systems
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: stable population; consistent tax base; focus shifts to optimization and quality-of-life improvements; aging infrastructure concern

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: increasing abandoned buildings; darkened structures; visible urban decay; deteriorating infrastructure appearance; reduced construction activity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: population loss; declining tax revenue; business closures; service degradation; desperation ordinances enacted

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: devastated zones with destroyed building graphics; rubble/wreckage visible; power outages affecting lighting; disrupted infrastructure networks
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: sudden population displacement; emergency budget requirements; accelerated aging of surviving infrastructure; temporary economic collapse

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: pollution visual indicators (if present); degraded environmental appearance; industrial zones showing environmental impact
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: population health decline; business relocation; regulatory pressure; ordinance requirements

environmental_storytelling.example: buildings display different construction eras through visual style; older power plants appear dated and trigger aging warnings; newer arcologies show futuristic design (fusion, satellite microwave plants)
water pipes, subways, and road tunnels visible as underground layers; allows player to read city circulatory system and identify bottlenecks or failures
elevation changes create natural city boundaries; hydroelectric dams can only place on waterfalls, forcing strategic infrastructure decisions; terrain variation communicates natural constraints
darkened buildings universally signal urban decay; player learns to identify at-risk neighborhoods through building darkness before population loss occurs

pre-written articles with variable names report on actual game events (aging power plants, disasters, technology adoption, opinion polls); creates illusion of city having autonomous narrative even in quiet moments

hovering over structures reveals structural data (land value, traffic, power consumption); encourages player to 'read' the city systematically; special queries (library 'Ruminate' essay) reward curiosity
residential, commercial, and industrial zones likely use distinct visual palettes; player learns to recognize sector composition at a glance

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: buildings appear well-maintained; high building density increases; positive newspaper headlines about population growth
buildings darken as abandonment spreads; visible density reduction; negative opinion poll headlines; migration to neighboring cities visible in road traffic
commercial zones densely populated; new businesses visible; high traffic on connecting roads; positive business news in newspapers
commercial lots empty; existing commercial buildings darken; reduced road traffic; business failure headlines in newspaper
factories fully rendered; power consumption high; pollution visible (environmental impact); trade traffic visible on highways and railways
factories appear shut down; power consumption drops; reduced traffic; buildings may show abandonment
fully rendered structure; query shows high power consumption percentage; no aging warnings; consistent power delivery to grid
structure appears degraded; query shows age-related warnings; power consumption drops; imminent failure signaled in newspaper
green interface highlights; funds available for expansion; emergency response capacity; ordinance enactment possible
red interface highlights; tax rate pressure increases; service cuts visible; ordinance options limited; bankruptcy risk looms; game-over condition approaching

notes: SimCity 2000 communicates world-state primarily through visual manifestation of infrastructure and building conditions, supplemented by query-tool information and newspaper narrative. The isometric perspective enables simultaneous viewing of multiple city systems (surface buildings, underground infrastructure, topography), creating visual literacy where player learns to 'read' city health at a glance. Faction identity is sector-based (residential/commercial/industrial/municipal) rather than opposed parties; visual differentiation occurs through building type and zone placement rather than color-coding or sigils. Danger signals are subtle (darkened buildings, query warnings) rather than aggressive, reinforcing simulation genre's emphasis on systemic observation. Environmental storytelling uses infrastructure interconnection visibility and aging mechanics to create narrative tension without explicit storytelling.
